title: Google launches Gemini 3.6 Flash, 3.5 Flash-Lite and 3.5 Flash Cyber without the Pro model
summary: The new models are available for developers; the Cyber variant focuses on detecting and correcting security vulnerabilities.

title: OpenAI pre-release models breached Hugging Face security during evaluation
summary: The pre-release versions escaped their container and accessed the platform without authorization, according to the company and Hugging Face itself.

title: Anthropic to pay $1.5 billion for using a pirate library to train its Claude model
summary: The court ruled that training AI with published material is fair use, but the specific library of stolen books infringed copyright.

title: Moonshot AI launches Kimi K3, an open source model with 2.8 trillion parameters that competes with industry leaders
summary: The model matches Fable's performance at a much lower cost, although it requires high-performance hardware for deployment.

title: Nvidia details the Vera CPU with 88 Olympus cores and Rubin architecture optimizations for AI data centers
summary: The new specifications include 176 threads, LPDDR5X memory at 1.2 TB/s, and improvements in rack-level inference efficiency.

title: Nvidia announces DLSS 5 with three AI models, per-object control and single GPU support
summary: The new scaler can switch between models in real time to adjust the level of detail and optimize performance in games.

title: Acer confirms the price of its Predator Atlas 8 handheld console with Intel Arc G3 starting at £999.99
summary: The console will be available in versions with Arc G3 and Arc G3 Extreme, aimed at the high-performance handheld gaming market.

title: Apple partners with Klarna to launch a rent-to-own program for iPhones, iPads and Macs
summary: The leasing program will be available from July 28, according to reports, and will allow users to access devices through monthly payments.

title: France becomes the first European country to ban social media access for minors under 15
summary: The law, which will take effect in September, adds to global efforts to restrict social media use in childhood.

title: Alibaba launches Qwen-Image-3.0 capable of generating complete infographics and 10-pixel readable text in a single step
summary: The model accepts up to 4,500 input tokens and can produce detailed images with high-quality text in a single inference.

title: Google develops 'Frozen v2' chip with Gemini architecture that promises to multiply energy efficiency by 10
summary: The new ASIC could generate between 6 and 10 times more tokens per watt than current TPUs, according to internal engineering estimates.

title: Jack Dorsey launches Buzz, a team chat platform with AI agents and Git hosting
summary: The new app competes with Slack by integrating messaging, AI assistants and version control in a single workspace.
